verbGlossary.CERTIFIES AGAINST: {source} provides worker credential or classification data checked against a regulatory compliance requirement (e.g., employee records certify against labor compliance for prevailing wage verification).
verbGlossary.CONFORMS TO: {source}-tracked material or shipment adheres to specifications validated through a regulatory plan review (e.g., material shipments conform to building department approved plans).
verbGlossary.COORDINATES WITH: {source} scheduling activity is aligned with {target} timeline to avoid work stoppages (e.g., scheduled work coordinates with inspection scheduling so trades are not covering work before inspector arrival).
verbGlossary.CORRELATES WITH: {source} metric is statistically associated with a regulatory measure, enabling root-cause analysis (e.g., delay frequency correlates with regulatory hold duration to quantify {target} impact on schedule).
verbGlossary.DEPLOYS UNDER: {source} equipment is mobilized on site under the authority of {target} that governs temporary installations (e.g., crane log entries deploy under a temporary structures permit).
verbGlossary.DISRUPTS: {source} condition causes a regulatory scheduling activity to be postponed or rescheduled (e.g., weather delays disrupt planned inspection scheduling windows).
verbGlossary.DOCUMENTS: {source} creates a contemporaneous record of {target} event occurring on site (e.g., daily log inspection entry documents an AHJ inspection visit and its outcome).
verbGlossary.EVIDENCES: {source} serves as supporting proof for a {target} or certification (e.g., weather logs evidence stormwater permit BMP activation, construction reports evidence certificate of completion readiness).
verbGlossary.FEEDS: {source} data flows into a regulatory metric or compliance document as source input (e.g., daily log inspection entries feed inspection pass rate calculations, daily reports feed compliance document completeness).
verbGlossary.LOGS ARRIVAL OF: {source} visitor tracking captures the on-site presence of a regulatory inspector for audit trail purposes (e.g., visitor log logs arrival of {target} inspector or special inspector).
verbGlossary.MEASURES AGAINST: {source} metric is evaluated relative to a regulatory {target} threshold or target (e.g., waste diversion rate measures against environmental permit recycling requirements).
verbGlossary.MOBILIZES UNDER: {source} crew deploys to perform work authorized by a specific trade or activity {target} (e.g., electrical crew mobilizes under a trade permit for rough-in work).
verbGlossary.OPERATES UNDER: {source} activity takes place within the constraints and conditions of a site-access {target} (e.g., delivery log entries operate under street/right-of-way permits that govern truck routing and staging).
verbGlossary.RECORDS: {source} captures the occurrence and duration of a regulatory-caused event (e.g., delay log entry records a regulatory hold stopping work on a specific day).
verbGlossary.REFERENCES: {source} cites or links to {target} or regulatory document for traceability (e.g., daily log header references the active building permit number, accident log references the site safety plan).
verbGlossary.REGISTERED UNDER: {source} equipment master data is associated with {target} that authorizes its presence on site (e.g., equipment master record registered under building permit for tower crane installation).
verbGlossary.REPORTS: {source} note narratively describes a regulatory condition or status observed in the field (e.g., daily log note reports {target} hold or regulatory status change encountered during the workday).
verbGlossary.RESUBMITTED TO: {source}-received plan revision is sent back to {target} for re-review and approval (e.g., plan revision receipt triggers resubmission to building department plan review).
verbGlossary.SAMPLED BY: {source}received on site are tested or sampled by a third-party inspector for {target} (e.g., concrete or steel material receipts sampled by third-party testing lab).
verbGlossary.SUBSTANTIATES: {source} provides documentary evidence that supports compliance with a regulatory {target} requirement (e.g., waste logs substantiate environmental permit disposal tracking, manpower logs substantiate labor compliance certified payroll).
verbGlossary.TRIGGERS: {source} event initiates a mandatory regulatory notification or filing (e.g., safety violation triggers an OSHA notification, accident log entry triggers OSHA reporting requirement).
verbGlossary.VIOLATES: {source}-documented condition represents a non-conformance against a regulatory safety plan (e.g., safety violation log entry violates the site safety plan requiring corrective action).
